name: Thai Barbecued Stuffed Prawns
recipeIngredient: 16 to 20 bamboo skewers, at least 8-inches or longer
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the bamboo skewers in water for at least 30 minutes.
2. Rinse the coriander bunch in cold water, removing sand by dunking it up and down. Drain thoroughly and spin dry.
3. Cut off the roots, including about 1 inch of stem above the root, and chop coarsely. Set aside.
4. Pound black pepper corns into a mortar or grind in a spice mill.
5. Add garlic, coriander roots, sugar, and salt to the peppercorns; pound or grind into a smooth paste.
6. Combine the spice paste, fish sauce, coriander leaves, and chicken; mix well.
7. Devein and butterfly the prawns by making a 1/2-inch deep cut through the shell along the back with kitchen scissors. Pull out the black vein (alimentary canal).
8. Rinse prawns under cold running water and dry thoroughly.
9. Thread the prawns lengthwise onto the skewers, starting from the tail end. Stuff each prawn with about a teaspoonful of chicken paste; smooth the surface.
10. Repeat with remaining prawns.
11. Lightly brush prawns with oil. Grill over medium-hot fire for 1 to 2 minutes on each side, or until they turn bright orange and feel firm to touch. They may also be broiled.
12. Serve with Sweet-Sour Cucumber Salad.
SWEET AND SOUR CUCUMBER SALAD:
- 4 tablespoons white vinegar
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 4 tablespoons water
- 1/2 cucumber, peeled and thinly sliced
- 1 teaspoon dried shrimp powder with chili (optional)
- 1/2 tablespoon fish sauce (nam pla)
- 2 teaspoons fresh coriander leaves, minced
Combine vinegar, sugar, salt, and water in a small bowl; stir until sugar and salt dissolve. Put cucumber slices in a shallow bowl, sprinkle with shrimp powder and fish sauce, then pour in the sweet vinegar to cover. Top with coriander leaves. Makes 8 servings.
